HO-Azure :: attack-path-focused Azure recon
context :: tenant=auto subscription=auto output=table

[chains] Correlating grouped chain evidence with conservative cross-command joins.
ho-azure chains

│high    │pivot-now│deploy-    │currently actionable│Poison repository azure-repos:customer-portal@refs/heads/main│exact app service: app-public-api│Current evidence shows you can poison this trusted input so it runs as Azure identity 'build-sp' against the exact App Service target, but not a separate direct sign-in as Azure identity 'build-sp'.│Current credentials can already poison a trusted input; HO-Azure already named the exact App Service target app-public-api; supporting ARM deployment history includes app-failed.│
│        │         │appservice-│                    │through repo-content,                                        │                                 │                                                                                                                                                                                                      │                                                                                                                                                                                  │

╰────────┴─────────┴───────────┴────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│note                                                                                                                                                                                   │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│This path trusts repository azure-repos:customer-portal@refs/heads/main.                                                                                                               │
│Current credentials can already modify that trusted input.                                                                                                                             │
│The resulting run would use Azure identity 'build-sp' when it makes changes in Azure.                                                                                                  │
│HO-Azure already ties this path to workload deployment reach, configuration change reach, recurring Azure execution, and secret-backed deployment support.                             │
│Additional visible deployment support around this path includes secret variables and publish profiles and deployment credentials.                                                      │
│This pipeline runs as Azure identity 'build-sp', which already has Owner across 2 visible scopes.                                                                                      │
│HO-Azure also sees a separate app trust path into that same Azure identity through app 'build-app'.                                                                                    │
│Visible target-side record: App Service 'app-public-api' publishes hostname 'app-public-api.azurewebsites.net', runs runtime 'NODE|20-lts', and uses managed identity (SystemAssigned).│
│Visible posture: public network access Enabled, HTTPS-only enabled, TLS 1.2, FTPS Disabled.                                                                                            │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

│note                                                                                                                                                                                                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│Automation account 'aa-hybrid-prod' combines webhook path can start runbook Redeploy-App under automation identity SystemAssigned, 6 published runbook(s), 4 schedule-backed run path(s), 1 Hybrid Worker reach point(s).│
│HO-Azure already ties this path to configuration change reach, recurring Azure execution, and secret-backed deployment support.                                                                                          │
│Additional visible deployment support around this account includes credentials and certificates and connections and encrypted variables.                                                                                 │
│Current role assignment Owner at subscription scope can edit runbook Redeploy-App or its webhook-backed execution boundary; current scope does not expose the webhook URI value.                                         │
│Azure identity 'aa-hybrid-prod-mi' already has Contributor across subscription-wide scope.                                                                                                                               │
│HO-Azure also sees a separate identity-control path into Azure identity 'ops-deploy-sp' through service principal 'aa-hybrid-prod'.                                                                                      │
│Visible App Service evidence keeps 1 candidate(s) in play; 1 keep public network access enabled and 1 carry managed identity.                                                                                            │

├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│This path trusts repository azure-repos:platform-api@refs/heads/main.                                                                                                             │
│If that trusted input is changed upstream, the resulting run would use Azure identity 'azurefox-lab-sp' when it makes changes in Azure.                                           │
│HO-Azure already ties this path to workload deployment reach, configuration change reach, recurring Azure execution, and secret-backed deployment support.                        │
│Additional visible deployment support around this path includes secret variables and signing keys and registry credentials and deployment credentials and Key Vault-backed inputs.│
│HO-Azure has not matched named Key Vault support (kv-prod-shared) to visible vault inventory.                                                                                     │
│This pipeline runs as Azure identity 'azurefox-lab-sp', which already has Owner across subscription-wide scope.                                                                   │
│HO-Azure also sees a separate identity-control path into Azure identity 'ops-deploy-sp' through service principal 'aa-hybrid-prod'.                                               │
│Visible AKS evidence keeps 2 candidate(s) in play; 1 keep private API endpoints, 1 keep workload identity enabled, and 1 still use service principal auth.                        │

│This path trusts repository github:infra-live@refs/heads/main.                                                                                                                                     │
│If that trusted input is changed upstream, the resulting run would use the Azure identity tied to this pipeline when it makes changes in Azure.                                                    │
│HO-Azure already ties this path to infrastructure deployment reach, configuration change reach, recurring Azure execution, and secret-backed deployment support.                                   │
│Additional visible deployment support around this path includes Key Vault-backed inputs.                                                                                                           │
│HO-Azure has not matched named Key Vault support (kv-platform-shared) to visible vault inventory.                                                                                                  │
│Visible ARM deployment history keeps 2 resource group deployment(s), 1 subscription deployment(s) in play across providers Microsoft.KeyVault, Microsoft.Network, Microsoft.Storage, Microsoft.Web.│

│note                                                                                                                                                                                  │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│Automation account 'aa-lab-quiet' does not on its own show a current-credential Azure change path, but it concentrates connections and encrypted variables around reusable automation.│
│Current role assignment Owner at subscription scope can edit runbook Lab-Maintenance or its schedule-backed execution boundary.                                                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Claim boundary: Can claim that the visible evidence suggests a controllable or nearly controllable Azure change path and can name or narrow the likely downstream footprint when the join is honest. Cannot claim successful execution or the exact downstream Azure change from current visible evidence alone.
